meta.name: Microsoft Security, Compliance & Identity (SC-900)
meta.description: Foundational security, compliance, and identity concepts for the SC-900 certification exam.

cards.definition: Authentication (AuthN) is the process of verifying who you are - confirming your identity through credentials such as a password, biometric, or certificate. Authorisation (AuthZ) is the process of determining what you are allowed to do - what resources you can access after your identity is confirmed. Authentication always happens before authorisation.
cards.hint: AuthN = proving who you are. AuthZ = deciding what you can do.

cards.definition: A security method requiring users to verify their identity using two or more factors from different categories: Something you know (password/PIN), Something you have (authenticator app, hardware token, phone), Something you are (fingerprint, face recognition). MFA dramatically reduces the risk of account compromise even if a password is stolen. Microsoft Entra ID enforces MFA via Conditional Access or security defaults.
cards.hint: Combining a password with a second proof - so stolen passwords alone aren't enough.

cards.definition: A Microsoft Entra ID feature that applies if-then policy rules to access requests: IF a user matches certain conditions (location, device compliance, risk level, app being accessed) THEN require MFA, block access, or allow. For example: block all access from outside the country OR require MFA for admin accounts. It is the primary Zero Trust enforcement tool in Microsoft 365.
cards.hint: Policy rules that evaluate who, what device, and where before granting access.

cards.definition: Microsoft Defender Extended Detection and Response (XDR) is a unified security platform that correlates threat signals across identities (Defender for Identity), endpoints (Defender for Endpoint), email (Defender for Office 365), and cloud apps (Defender for Cloud Apps). It provides a single incident view with automated investigation and response (AIR) to reduce mean time to detect and respond to attacks.
cards.hint: One dashboard that connects threat signals from every corner of your Microsoft environment.

cards.definition: A cloud-native Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) and Security Orchestration, Automation and Response (SOAR) service. It collects log data from across your entire organisation (Azure, Microsoft 365, on-premises, multi-cloud), uses AI to detect threats, investigates incidents with built-in dashboards, and automates responses via Logic Apps playbooks.
cards.hint: Azure's cloud-native SIEM - collecting logs everywhere, detecting threats with AI, automating responses.

cards.definition: Microsoft's unified data governance and compliance platform. Covers: Information Protection (sensitivity labels to classify/encrypt data), Data Loss Prevention (prevent leakage of sensitive info), Records Management (legal retention schedules), eDiscovery (legal hold and content search), Audit (activity logs), and Communication Compliance. Formerly branded as Microsoft 365 Compliance.
cards.hint: Classify, protect, retain, and audit your organisation's data to meet compliance requirements.

cards.definition: A Microsoft Entra ID feature that manages, controls, and monitors access to important (privileged) roles. Instead of permanently assigning admin roles, PIM makes roles eligible - admins activate them just-in-time for a limited duration, requiring approval and MFA. Every activation is audited. Reduces the attack surface by ensuring powerful permissions are only active when genuinely needed.
cards.hint: Admin rights that are dormant by default and activated just-in-time with an audit trail.

cards.definition: A risk-based identity protection service that uses Microsoft's threat intelligence to detect suspicious sign-in behaviours - such as sign-ins from anonymous IPs, impossible travel (logging in from two distant countries within minutes), or leaked credentials found on the dark web. Assigns a risk score (low/medium/high) and can automatically block or require MFA based on risk level via Conditional Access.
cards.hint: AI that watches for suspicious login patterns and blocks risky sign-ins automatically.

cards.definition: A layered security strategy where multiple controls protect data, so that if one layer is breached, subsequent layers continue to provide protection. The seven layers from outside in: Physical → Perimeter (DDoS, firewall) → Network (segmentation, NSGs) → Compute (patch OS, antivirus) → Application (secure code, WAF) → Data (encryption, access control). No single control is sufficient.
cards.hint: Security like an onion - an attacker must peel through many layers to reach your data.

cards.definition: A modern security model built on three core principles: Verify explicitly (always authenticate and authorise using all available data points - identity, location, device, service, workload), Use least privilege access (limit user access with just-in-time and just-enough-access), and Assume breach (minimise blast radius; segment access; verify end-to-end encryption). Replaces the outdated "trusted network perimeter" model.
cards.hint: Never trust, always verify - from every user, every device, every time.

cards.definition: Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service. Provides authentication, authorization, SSO, MFA, Conditional Access, identity governance, and lifecycle management. Every Microsoft 365 tenant uses Entra ID as its identity backbone.
cards.hint: The identity provider behind Microsoft 365 and Azure.

cards.definition: Allows users to authenticate once and access multiple applications without re-entering credentials. Reduces password fatigue and improves security. Entra ID supports SSO for Microsoft 365, Azure, and thousands of SaaS apps.
cards.hint: Sign in once, access everything.

cards.definition: Authentication without passwords using methods such as Windows Hello for Business, Microsoft Authenticator app, and FIDO2 security keys. Reduces phishing and credential theft risks.
cards.hint: Log in without passwords - safer and easier.

cards.definition: A Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) that provides visibility into cloud app usage, detects risky behavior, enforces session controls, and protects data across SaaS apps. Integrates with Conditional Access.
cards.hint: CASB that monitors and controls cloud app usage.

cards.definition: A security solution that monitors on-premises Active Directory for suspicious activities such as lateral movement, credential theft, and privilege escalation. Uses behavioral analytics to detect attacks.
cards.hint: Protects on-prem AD from identity-based attacks.

cards.definition: An endpoint protection platform providing antivirus, EDR, vulnerability management, attack surface reduction, and automated investigation. Protects Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android devices.
cards.hint: Advanced security for laptops, desktops, and mobile devices.

cards.definition: Protects email, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams from phishing, malware, and malicious links. Includes Safe Links, Safe Attachments, anti-phishing policies, and attack simulation training.
cards.hint: Email and collaboration protection.

cards.definition: Preconfigured security settings in Entra ID that enforce MFA, block legacy authentication, and enable basic protections. Designed for small organizations without complex security needs.
cards.hint: One-click baseline security for all tenants.

cards.definition: Controls identity lifecycle, access reviews, entitlement management, and privileged access. Ensures users have the right access at the right time and removes unnecessary permissions.
cards.hint: Govern who has access and for how long.

cards.definition: A feature in Entra ID that bundles resources (apps, groups, SharePoint sites) into access packages. Users request access, and approvals, expiration, and reviews are automated.
cards.hint: Self-service access with automated approvals.

cards.definition: Periodic reviews of user access to groups, apps, and roles. Ensures users retain only the access they need. Required for Zero Trust and compliance frameworks.
cards.hint: Regularly verify who should still have access.

cards.definition: Policies that detect and prevent sharing of sensitive information across email, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams. Can block, warn, or audit risky actions.
cards.hint: Stops sensitive data from leaking.

cards.definition: Uses sensitivity labels to classify and protect documents and emails. Labels can apply encryption, watermarking, access restrictions, and auto-classification.
cards.hint: Classify and protect data with labels.

cards.definition: Tools for legal teams to search, preserve, and export content across Microsoft 365. Supports legal hold, case management, and advanced search queries.
cards.hint: Search and preserve data for legal investigations.

cards.definition: Provides logs of user and admin activities across Microsoft 365. Advanced Audit (E5) includes longer retention and high-value events such as mail read events.
cards.hint: Track who did what and when.

cards.definition: A measurement of an organization's security posture. Provides prioritized recommendations such as enabling MFA, blocking legacy authentication, and configuring DLP.
cards.hint: Your security grade with recommended improvements.

cards.definition: A measurement of how well an organization meets regulatory requirements. Provides improvement actions mapped to standards like GDPR, ISO 27001, and HIPAA.
cards.hint: Your compliance grade with actionable steps.

cards.definition: Microsoft secures the cloud infrastructure; customers secure their data, identities, devices, and access policies. Responsibilities vary by service type (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS).
cards.hint: Microsoft secures the cloud; you secure your data.

cards.definition: Attackers attempt to bypass security controls using methods like disabling antivirus, using legitimate tools (living-off-the-land), or exploiting misconfigurations. Defender XDR detects these behaviors.
cards.hint: How attackers try to hide from security tools.

cards.definition: Microsoft Purview detects risky user behavior such as data exfiltration, unusual file downloads, or policy violations. Helps organizations mitigate insider threats.
cards.hint: Detects risky behavior from users inside the organization.

cards.definition: A decentralized identity solution that allows users to present verifiable credentials (e.g., employment status, certifications) without exposing unnecessary personal data. Organizations issue credentials, and users control when and where they are shared.
cards.hint: Digital credentials users fully control.

cards.definition: A solution for managing external users such as customers and partners. Supports authentication via social accounts, email OTP, or enterprise identity providers. Used for B2B and B2C scenarios.
cards.hint: Identity management for customers and partners.

cards.definition: The process of provisioning, updating, and deprovisioning user accounts. Includes automated onboarding, role assignment, access reviews, and offboarding to reduce security risks.
cards.hint: Manage user accounts from joiner → mover → leaver.

cards.definition: Provides managed domain services like LDAP, Kerberos, and NTLM authentication without deploying domain controllers. Useful for legacy apps that require traditional AD.
cards.hint: Domain services in the cloud without domain controllers.

cards.definition: A cloud security posture management (CSPM) and workload protection platform for Azure, AWS, and GCP. Provides security recommendations, compliance assessments, and threat protection for cloud workloads.
cards.hint: Secure your cloud resources across Azure, AWS, and GCP.

cards.definition: A measurement of endpoint security posture in Defender for Endpoint. Provides recommendations such as enabling attack surface reduction rules, updating OS versions, and improving antivirus settings.
cards.hint: A security grade for your devices.

cards.definition: A set of rules in Defender for Endpoint that block risky behaviors such as launching executables from email, running untrusted scripts, or abusing Office macros.
cards.hint: Reduce the ways attackers can enter your system.

cards.definition: Secure Score recommendations are grouped into Identity, Device, Apps, Data, and Infrastructure. Each category provides prioritized actions to improve security posture.
cards.hint: Security improvements grouped by identity, devices, apps, data, and infrastructure.

cards.definition: Detects risky user behavior such as data exfiltration, unusual downloads, or policy violations. Uses machine learning to identify potential insider threats.
cards.hint: Detects risky actions from internal users.

cards.definition: Monitors internal communications (email, Teams, Yammer) for policy violations such as harassment, sensitive data sharing, or regulatory breaches. Alerts reviewers for investigation.
cards.hint: Monitor communication channels for policy violations.

cards.definition: Manages retention schedules, regulatory record declarations, and disposition reviews. Ensures content is kept or deleted according to legal requirements.
cards.hint: Retention and deletion rules for official records.

cards.definition: Automatically scans and classifies data across cloud and on-premises systems. Builds a unified map of data assets for governance and compliance.
cards.hint: A searchable map of all your organization's data.

cards.definition: Labels classify and protect data with encryption, watermarking, and access restrictions. Labels travel with the data across devices and apps.
cards.hint: Classify and protect documents and emails.

cards.definition: Automates retention and deletion of data based on policies. Helps organizations meet regulatory and business requirements for data lifecycle.
cards.hint: Automate how long data is kept or deleted.

cards.definition: Provides real-time global threat intelligence from Microsoft's security research teams. Helps analysts understand attacker tools, infrastructure, and techniques.
cards.hint: Threat intelligence to understand attacker behavior.

cards.definition: Automated workflows built using Logic Apps that respond to security incidents. Examples: block an IP, disable a user, notify SOC teams.
cards.hint: Automated incident response workflows.

cards.definition: Rules that detect threats by analyzing log data. Includes scheduled rules, machine learning rules, and Microsoft-provided templates.
cards.hint: Rules that detect suspicious activity in logs.

cards.definition: Interactive dashboards for visualizing security data. Used for investigations, monitoring, and reporting.
cards.hint: Custom dashboards for security insights.

cards.definition: KQL-based queries used to proactively search for threats across log data. Helps analysts identify anomalies and attacker behavior.
cards.hint: Proactive threat hunting using KQL.

cards.definition: A set of best practices and security controls for securing cloud workloads. Integrated into Defender for Cloud to assess compliance.
cards.hint: Microsoft's recommended security baseline for cloud workloads.
